categories.name: Testing
categories.summary: 86 test files, 643 test cases, 139% file ratio (86 tests / 62 source files)

subcategories.name: Coverage ratio
subcategories.summary: 86 test files for 62 source files (139% ratio). Good unit and browser test coverage.
locations: lib/helpers/composeSignals.js
lib/helpers/trackStream.js
lib/helpers/resolveConfig.js

subcategories.name: Edge case depth
subcategories.summary: 643 test cases including prototype pollution tests, cancellation, timeouts, redirects
subcategories.issuesDescription: missing edge case tests for HTTP/2 session management and data URI handling

subcategories.name: Test quality
subcategories.summary: Tests use vitest with browser and unit splits. Some tests lack assertion depth.

categories.name: Security
categories.summary: Prototype pollution guards present, XSRF protection, proxy auth handling. Cookie regex injection risk.

subcategories.name: Secrets & env vars
subcategories.summary: No hardcoded secrets. Proxy auth handled via config objects.

subcategories.name: Input validation
subcategories.summary: Prototype pollution protection in merge() and mergeConfig(). Cookie name used in regex without escaping.
locations: lib/helpers/cookies.js:37 — cookie name injected into RegExp without escaping, enabling ReDoS or cookie hijacking
lib/adapters/http.js:186 — proxy.auth object checked but proxy.hostname not validated against SSRF
lib/core/buildFullPath.js:18 — loose equality (==) check on allowAbsoluteUrls can lead to unexpected coercion

subcategories.name: Auth & XSRF protection
subcategories.summary: Built-in XSRF cookie/header support. Same-origin check present but IE-specific bypass.
locations: lib/helpers/isURLSameOrigin.js:9 — MSIE detection bypasses port check in same-origin validation
lib/adapters/http.js:612 — URL credentials (parsed.username/password) silently override auth config

categories.name: Type Safety
categories.summary: JavaScript source with TypeScript declaration files (.d.ts). No strict type checking on source.

subcategories.name: Strict config
subcategories.summary: TypeScript declarations exist (index.d.ts, index.d.cts) but source is JavaScript only. tsconfig.json present but minimal.
locations: tsconfig.json — exists but not used for source compilation

subcategories.name: Any type count
subcategories.summary: 4 JSDoc annotations use {any} type. Declaration files have proper typing.
subcategories.issuesDescription: uses of any/untyped patterns in JSDoc
locations: lib/helpers/toFormData.js:72 — @param {Object<any, any>}
lib/helpers/toFormData.js:85 — @param {Object<string, any>}
lib/core/mergeConfig.js:7 — @param {Object<string, any>}
lib/utils.js:405 — generic Object params without type constraints

categories.name: Error Handling
categories.summary: Custom AxiosError class with codes. Some swallowed errors and console.warn in production code.

subcategories.name: Error class design
subcategories.summary: Well-designed AxiosError with error codes (ETIMEDOUT, ERR_NETWORK, etc.), toJSON, and .from() factory.

subcategories.name: Swallowed errors
subcategories.summary: Empty catch blocks and console.warn used for error handling in production paths.
locations: lib/adapters/http.js:530 — empty catch block (catch (e) {}) when getting FormData content length
lib/adapters/http.js:379 — console.warn('emit error', err) swallows abort emission errors
lib/core/Axios.js:52 — catch (e) {} silently ignores stack trace augmentation failures
lib/helpers/validator.js:51 — console.warn for deprecated options instead of throwing

subcategories.name: Error recovery
subcategories.summary: Good error wrapping via AxiosError.from(). Missing cleanup in some error paths.
locations: lib/adapters/http.js:624 — custom Error created without AxiosError wrapping loses error code
lib/adapters/http.js:693 — req.setTimeout(0) on no-timeout path may conflict with keep-alive

categories.name: Performance
categories.summary: HTTP/2 session pooling, stream pipeline support. God-file http.js adapter is 951 lines.

subcategories.name: Bundle efficiency
subcategories.summary: Tree-shakeable ESM exports. utils.js (919 lines) is a kitchen-sink module.
locations: lib/utils.js — 919-line utility module with 60+ exports, not tree-shakeable granularly
lib/adapters/http.js — 951-line god file handling HTTP/1, HTTP/2, data URIs, proxies, compression

subcategories.name: Runtime performance
subcategories.summary: HTTP/2 session reuse with timeout. util.isDeepStrictEqual used for session matching (potentially slow).
locations: lib/adapters/http.js:82 — util.isDeepStrictEqual for HTTP/2 session matching on every request
lib/utils.js:315 — findKey iterates all keys to do case-insensitive lookup (O(n) per call, called frequently)
lib/core/AxiosHeaders.js:249 — normalize() calls findKey in a loop (O(n²) for headers)

subcategories.name: Memory management
subcategories.summary: Response buffered in memory by default. No streaming by default for large responses.
locations: lib/adapters/http.js:583 — responseBuffer array accumulates all chunks in memory before concatenation
lib/adapters/http.js:70 — Http2Sessions object never has entries GC'd if session.close events are missed

categories.name: Code Quality
categories.summary: 32 eslint-disable comments. Multiple 'hotfix' TODOs. Duplicate global detection in utils.js.

subcategories.name: Code organization
subcategories.summary: Clear module separation (core/adapters/helpers). http.js adapter is a god file needing decomposition.
locations: lib/adapters/http.js — 951 lines with HTTP/1, HTTP/2, data URI, proxy, compression all in one file
lib/utils.js — 919 lines of mixed utility functions (type checks, iteration, merging, platform detection)
lib/helpers/toFormData.js — 'temporary hotfix' comment (line 5) indicates unresolved circular reference

subcategories.name: Code hygiene
subcategories.summary: 32 eslint-disable comments. 4 'hotfix' TODOs still in production code.
locations: lib/utils.js:295 — duplicate getGlobal/getGlobalThis logic (lines 272 and 295 define _global separately)
lib/core/AxiosHeaders.js:326 — static accessor() has assignment bug: this[$internals] = this[$internals] = {...}
lib/adapters/http.js:241 — '// temporary hotfix' comment with no linked issue
lib/adapters/http.js:354 — '// hotfix to support opt.all option' — unclear if still needed
lib/core/AxiosHeaders.js:333 — '// reserved names hotfix' — no explanation of what's reserved

subcategories.name: Maintainability
subcategories.summary: Good JSDoc coverage. Some complex nested closures hard to follow.
locations: lib/adapters/http.js:244-268 — wrapAsync is a complex promise wrapper with non-obvious done/onDone lifecycle
lib/adapters/fetch.js:288-312 — seedCache uses nested Map structure that's hard to reason about
lib/core/Axios.js:136-173 — interceptor chain execution has two completely different code paths

issues.description: Cookie name injected directly into RegExp constructor without escaping special characters. An attacker-controlled cookie name could cause ReDoS or match unintended cookies.
issues.suggestedFix: Escape regex special characters in the cookie name: name.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&')

issues.description: Proxy hostname from config is used without validation. If user-supplied, could enable SSRF by routing requests through attacker-controlled proxies.
issues.suggestedFix: Validate proxy hostname against an allowlist or at minimum check for private/internal IP ranges.

issues.description: Same-origin check skips port comparison when User-Agent matches MSIE/Trident. This weakens XSRF protection on legacy browsers.
issues.suggestedFix: Remove the MSIE special case or document why port mismatch is acceptable for IE.

issues.description: URL-embedded credentials (parsed.username/password) silently override config.auth. This can cause credential confusion if URLs contain unexpected auth segments.
issues.suggestedFix: Log a warning or throw when both config.auth and URL credentials are present.

issues.description: Loose equality (== false) used for allowAbsoluteUrls check. Values like 0, '', or null would match unexpectedly.
issues.suggestedFix: Use strict equality (=== false) instead of loose equality.

issues.description: Empty catch block when getting FormData content length. Silently swallows errors that could indicate corrupted form data.
issues.suggestedFix: At minimum log the error, or set content-length to undefined explicitly to signal unknown length.

issues.description: Abort emission error caught and logged via console.warn. In production, this error is silently lost.
issues.suggestedFix: Emit the error through the reject path or use a debug logger that can be disabled in production.

issues.description: Custom Error created for URL build failures without wrapping in AxiosError. Loses error code classification.
issues.suggestedFix: Use AxiosError.from(err, AxiosError.ERR_BAD_REQUEST, config) for consistent error handling.

issues.description: Stack trace augmentation failure silently caught. While intentional, it masks issues with non-writable stack properties.
issues.suggestedFix: Add a debug-mode log for this case to aid debugging.

issues.description: util.isDeepStrictEqual called on every HTTP/2 request to find matching sessions. This is O(n*m) where n is sessions and m is options depth.
issues.suggestedFix: Hash the options object or use a simpler key comparison for session matching.

issues.description: findKey does case-insensitive linear search through all object keys. Called frequently from AxiosHeaders methods.
issues.suggestedFix: Maintain an internal lowercase-to-actual-key Map in AxiosHeaders for O(1) lookups.

issues.description: normalize() calls findKey in a loop over all headers, resulting in O(n²) complexity.
issues.suggestedFix: Build a normalized key map once and use it for all lookups during normalization.

issues.description: Duplicate global detection: getGlobal() defined at line 272 and _global at line 295 with identical logic.
issues.suggestedFix: Remove one duplicate and reuse the same reference throughout.

issues.description: Double assignment in static accessor(): this[$internals] = this[$internals] = {...} always overwrites. Should be this[$internals] ||= {...}.
issues.suggestedFix: Use this[$internals] = this[$internals] || { accessors: {} } to preserve existing accessors.

issues.description: 951-line god file handling HTTP/1.x, HTTP/2, data URIs, proxy configuration, response decompression, and progress tracking.
issues.suggestedFix: Decompose into separate modules: http1Adapter, http2Adapter, dataUriHandler, proxyResolver, decompressionPipeline.

issues.description: 919-line kitchen-sink utility module with 60+ exports. Not granularly tree-shakeable.
issues.suggestedFix: Split into focused utility modules: typeChecks.js, iteration.js, merge.js, platform.js.

issues.description: '// temporary hotfix' comment with no linked issue or explanation of what it fixes.
issues.suggestedFix: Either resolve the hotfix properly or link to a GitHub issue tracking the underlying problem.

issues.description: seedCache uses a nested Map structure for memoization that's hard to reason about and debug.
issues.suggestedFix: Use a composite key string (e.g., JSON.stringify of seed identities) for a flat Map lookup.

issues.description: '// temporary hotfix to avoid circular references' — indicates architectural issue that was never resolved.
issues.suggestedFix: Refactor AxiosURLSearchParams to break the circular dependency properly.

issues.description: Http2Sessions.sessions object grows unboundedly if session 'close' events are missed. No TTL or max-size enforcement.
issues.suggestedFix: Add a periodic cleanup sweep or max-sessions limit to prevent memory leaks in long-running processes.

issues.description: '// hotfix to support opt.all option which is required for node 20.x' — may no longer be needed if Node 18 is dropped.
issues.suggestedFix: Verify if this hotfix is still needed for supported Node.js versions and remove if not.

issues.description: req.setTimeout(0) called when no timeout configured. The semantics of setTimeout(0) are 'disable timeout' but this is unintuitive.
issues.suggestedFix: Add a comment explaining why setTimeout(0) is used, or skip the call entirely when no timeout is set.

issues.description: getGlobal() function returns globalThis/self/window/global — globalThis is standard and sufficient for all modern targets.
issues.suggestedFix: Use globalThis directly since axios already requires Node 14+ which supports it.

issues.description: supportsRequestStream uses a test() helper that swallows all errors. If the test has side effects, errors are silently lost.
issues.suggestedFix: At minimum, log errors in development mode for debuggability.

issues.description: Two completely different execution paths for synchronous vs async interceptors. Increases maintenance burden and potential for behavior divergence.
issues.suggestedFix: Consider unifying into a single async path or documenting the behavioral differences clearly.
